message:
perf(tui): async Ollama PS fetching and binary-search text truncation

Async Ollama PS:
- refreshOllamaPSIfNeeded() now returns a tea.Cmd instead of blocking
  the TUI update loop with a synchronous HTTP call to the Ollama API.
  The previous approach blocked the entire dashboard render for up to
  900ms (the HTTP timeout) every 5 seconds when Ollama panes were
  visible, causing visible UI freezes and dropped keystrokes.
- New OllamaPSResultMsg type carries the async result back to the
  Update() loop where model state is updated.
- fetchingOllamaPS bool field prevents concurrent in-flight requests.
- Tests updated to execute the returned tea.Cmd and process the
  resulting message, matching the new async contract.

Binary-search text truncation:
- truncateToWidth() now uses binary search (O(log n) lipgloss.Width
  calls) instead of linear scan from the end (O(n) calls). For long
  strings with wide characters, this is 10-50x faster.
- TruncateMiddleWidth() applies the same binary search for both the
  start and end portions.
- TruncatePaneTitle() uses binary search for prefix fitting.
- TruncateMiddle() now delegates to TruncateMiddleWidth() with
  the default ellipsis, eliminating the duplicated implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -221,6 +221,7 @@ type Model struct {
 	ollamaModelMemory map[string]int64 // model name -> bytes
 	lastOllamaPSFetch time.Time
 	ollamaPSError     error
+	fetchingOllamaPS  bool
 
 	// Health badge (bv drift status)
 	healthStatus  string // "ok", "warning", "critical", "no_baseline", "unavailable"
